display.purpose: Hero trip titles over the dark photographic threshold.

label.purpose: Sentence-case eyebrows, field labels, meta keys (ink-dim/faint). Never a tracked-uppercase block.

shadows.name: print-frame
shadows.purpose: Hairline frame around photographs, gallery-print style. The only 'elevation' on images.

motion.name: ease-cinematic
motion.purpose: House ease-out for reveals, hover, and the hero cross-fade.

motion.name: hero-crossfade
motion.purpose: Trip image swap behind the gallery index.

motion.name: hero-push
motion.purpose: Slow 1.05x scale push on the active hero image.

motion.name: reveal
motion.purpose: Scroll-in reveal. Visible by default; JS-gated entrance with a transition-free safety net.

components.name: Gallery Index Title
components.description: Signature hero list item: bold grotesque trip title, obsession word in electric pink, tabular date. Over the dark photographic threshold.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Solid deep-pink near-square pill. One of only two resting uses of pink.

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Outlined near-square pill for the hero nav and secondary actions.

components.name: Interest Chip
components.description: Multi-select interest pill (near-square), flat on the ground. Active state fills ink, not pink.

components.name: Underline Field
components.description: Boxless field flat on the ground: ink text over a bottom rule, label above.

narrative.overview: freaks is a daylit gallery painted in two soft tones, pale lemon and cool mist, entered through and closed by a dark photographic threshold. The page opens on a full-bleed, scrimmed photograph with the trip names over it (the dark hero), settles into the bright two-tone gallery (lemon and mist sections alternating, photographs framed like prints, near-black type), and closes on a dark footer that bookends the hero. Color is held in reserve: one electric pink appears only twice at rest, the obsession word in a trip title and the primary action button. Everywhere else is grounds and ink.
keyCharacteristics: Two soft grounds, pale lemon (primary) and cool mist (alternating)
Dark photographic threshold at both ends: the hero and the footer bookend the page
Pink used twice at rest only: the obsession mark and the primary CTA
A single grotesque (Bricolage Grotesque) across weights, no serif, no mono
Photographs framed like prints (hairline inset); flat surfaces, no shadows
Near-square 3px corners; specificity and tabular numbers as the luxury

rules.name: The Two-Highlights Rule
rules.body: Pink appears at rest in exactly two places: the obsession word in a trip title (bright) and the primary action button (deep). Everywhere else, including eyebrows, links, benchmark labels, and active chips, is ink. Pink may additionally carry interaction states (focus ring, link hover).

rules.name: The Two-Grounds-and-Bookends Rule
rules.body: The body alternates two soft grounds, lemon and mist; the hero and footer are the one dark tone, bookending the page. No third ground color, no pink surface. Sections separate by ground and space, never by a rule or gradient.

rules.name: The Obsession-in-Pink Rule
rules.body: In any trip title, the thing you're obsessed with is bright pink at weight 700 and the place is ink/off-white. One of only two resting uses of pink; always large display type.

rules.name: The Tabular-Truth Rule
rules.body: All comparable numbers (benchmarks, distances, times, dates, prices) use tabular figures. The data is the proof of seriousness; it aligns.

rules.name: The Print-Frame Rule
rules.body: Photographs are framed like gallery prints with a 1px inset hairline, never a drop shadow. For separation, change ground or frame; do not lift.

dos: Do carry the page on two soft grounds (lemon primary, mist alternating) and bookend it with the dark hero and footer.
Do keep pink to exactly two resting uses: the obsession mark and the primary CTA.
Do mark the obsession word in bright pink + weight 700; keep the place in ink/off-white.
Do use a single typeface (Bricolage Grotesque) across weights.
Do frame photographs like prints with a hairline inset, and keep surfaces flat.
Do set every comparable number in tabular figures.
Do keep corners near-square: 3px pills, 10px modal.
donts: Don't let pink leak back into eyebrows, links, surfaces, or active chips at rest; those are ink.

Don't drift into the warm/dark register (orange-on-black reads Halloween / Strava) or back into the editorial-typographic lane: no serif italics, no mono labels, no hairline-rule eyebrows, no cream paper.
Don't add a second accent hue, a third ground color, or a second typeface.
Don't round the pills back into full lozenges; the angular 3px corner is the brand.
Don't build a generic travel-operator layout: card grids, star ratings, 'book now' clutter, stock-smile photography.
Don't drift into hustle/retreat-bro wellness, corporate SaaS scaffolding, or mass-luxury influencer travel.
Don't use em dashes in copy; en dashes for numeric ranges only.
